candidates.page_title: Dakin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dakin oxidation describing conversion of ortho- or para-hydroxylated, alkoxylated, or amino-substituted aromatic aldehydes and ketones to substituted phenols using hydrogen peroxide or organic peracids under basic conditions through O-acylphenol intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: hydroperoxide addition, 1,2-aryl shift, O-acylphenol intermediates, substituted phenol formation from activated aromatic carbonyl compounds.

candidates.page_title: Dakin-West Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dakin-West reaction describing conversion of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones by treatment with acid anhydrides and base through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates, followed by acyl transfer and decarboxylation.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: amino-acid N-acylation, mixed anhydride formation, oxazolone intermediates, alpha-acylamino ketone formation.

candidates.page_title: Danheiser Benzannulation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Danheiser benzannulation describing conversion of cyclobutenones or alpha-diazoketones and activated heterosubstituted alkynes into highly substituted aromatic rings through vinylketene generation, cycloaddition, electrocyclic ring opening, and dienylketene rearrangement.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: cyclobutenone annulation, vinylketene intermediates, [2+2] cycloaddition/electrocyclic cascade, highly substituted aromatic ring synthesis.

candidates.page_title: Danheiser Cyclopentene Annulation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Danheiser cyclopentene annulation describing TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with electron-deficient alkenes (allenophiles) to form highly substituted cyclopentenes through allylic cation capture and 1,2-silyl shift pathways.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: TiCl4 activation of enones, trimethylsilylallene addition, allylic carbocation intermediates, 1,2-silyl shift, highly substituted cyclopentene formation.

candidates.page_title: Danishefsky's Diene Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Danishefsky's diene cycloaddition describing the electron-rich 1-methoxy-3-trimethylsilyloxy-1,3-butadiene as a versatile heterodiene in Lewis-acid-mediated or thermal [4+2] cycloadditions, often proceeding through Mukaiyama-aldol or Diels–Alder manifolds to oxygenated cyclohexenones and dihydropyrones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Danishefsky diene, hetero Diels–Alder cycloaddition, Mukaiyama aldol pathway, Lewis-acid activation, oxygenated six-membered rings.

candidates.page_title: Darzens Glycidic Ester Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Darzens glycidic ester condensation describing base-promoted reaction of aldehydes or ketones with alpha-halo esters, nitriles, sulfones, or amides to form glycidic esters or related epoxides through enolate addition and intramolecular SN2 ring closure.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: alpha-halo carbonyl pronucleophiles, Darzens condensation, halohydrin/enolate intermediates, glycidic ester formation, cis/trans epoxide control.

candidates.page_title: Davis' Oxaziridine Oxidations – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Davis' oxaziridine oxidations describing N-sulfonyloxaziridines as neutral oxygen-transfer reagents for sulfide and selenide oxidation, amine N-oxidation, and especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates to acyloins via SN2-type oxygen transfer.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxaziridine oxygen transfer, Davis reagent family, enolate alpha-hydroxylation, sulfide oxidation, non-chelated transition-state control.

candidates.page_title: De Mayo Cycloaddition (Enone-Alkene [2+2] Photocycloaddition) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the De Mayo cycloaddition describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes to generate cyclobutanes or, via beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates and retro-aldol fragmentation, 1,5-diketones. The page emphasizes triplet-state exciplexes, 1,4-biradicals, and topology-dependent regioselectivity.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: triplet enone photochemistry, enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition, beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates, retro-aldol fragmentation, substituted cyclobutane formation.

candidates.page_title: Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangements describing nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or 1-aminomethylcycloalkanols to the corresponding cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through diazonium-ion formation and 1,2-alkyl migration.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: nitrosation to diazonium ions, ring expansion by 1,2-alkyl shift, Demjanov versus Tiffeneau-Demjanov substrate classes, competitive rearrangement pathways.

candidates.page_title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dess-Martin oxidation describing Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP), a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, as a mild and selective oxidant for 1° and 2° alcohols and oximes to the corresponding carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: IBX to DMP reagent preparation, hypervalent iodine oxidation, mild neutral conditions, aldehyde/ketone formation, alkoxyperiodinane intermediates.

candidates.page_title: Dieckmann Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dieckmann condensation describing intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters and the related Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization of dinitriles to enamino nitriles under strong base.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: intramolecular Claisen condensation, cyclic beta-keto ester formation, Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization, reversible enolate-driven ring closure, ring-size effects.

candidates.page_title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement describing acid- or base-promoted migration of alkyl groups in cyclohexadienones to aromatic phenols, including ortho- and para-dienone cases and sequential 1,2-shift pathways that deliver highly substituted phenols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: dienone rearomatization, alkyl migration, ortho versus para substitution patterns, 1,2-shift cascades, acid-catalyzed rearrangement of santonin-type frameworks.

candidates.page_title: Dimroth Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dimroth rearrangement describing ring-opening / ring-closing isomerization of heterocycles in which endocyclic or exocyclic heteroatoms and attached substituents are translocated, including Type I fused-ring and Type II exo/endocyclic heterocycle variants.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: ANRORC-type ring opening and reclosure, heteroatom translocation, nucleophile/base/heat promotion, and rearrangement control by ring size and substituent electronics.

candidates.page_title: Doering-Laflamme Allene Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Doering-Laflamme allene synthesis describing two-step one-carbon homologation of alkenes through dihalocarbene addition to form geminal dihalocyclopropanes followed by metal-mediated rearrangement to acyclic or cyclic allenes.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: in situ dihalocarbene generation from haloform, cyclopropanation of alkenes, metal–halogen exchange, and cyclopropylidene-to-allene rearrangement.

candidates.page_title: Dötz Benzannulation Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Dötz benzannulation reaction describing formal [3+2+1] annulation of Fischer-type chromium carbene complexes with alkynes to furnish hydroquinone or phenol derivatives after carbon monoxide loss and benzannulation.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: chromium Fischer carbene preparation, alkyne insertion, carbon monoxide extrusion, regiochemical control of benzannulation, and oxidative unveiling of hydroquinone products.

candidates.page_title: Enders SAMP/RAMP Hydrazone Alkylation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Enders SAMP/RAMP hydrazone alkylation describing asymmetric alpha-alkylation of ketones or aldehydes through chiral SAMP/RAMP hydrazone auxiliaries, azaenolate formation, and stereoselective electrophile addition.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: hydrazone formation from carbonyl compounds, LDA-mediated deprotonation, chiral auxiliary-controlled alkylation, and regeneration / diversification of the alkylated carbonyl products.

candidates.page_title: Enyne Metathesis – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for enyne metathesis describing catalyst-controlled redistribution of carbon–carbon multiple bonds between an alkene and an alkyne to form conjugated 1,3-dienes, including ring-closing and cross-enyne metathesis manifolds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Grubbs-type ruthenium carbene catalysis, ring-closing versus cross-enyne modes, 1,n-enyne substrates, and mechanistic divergence through metallacyclobutene intermediates.

candidates.page_title: Eschenmoser Methenylation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Eschenmoser methenylation describing installation of an exocyclic methylene equivalent at the alpha-position of carbonyl compounds by reaction of enolates with Eschenmoser’s salt followed by elimination from the resulting aminomethylated intermediate.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: enolate trapping with Eschenmoser’s salt, formation of alpha-(dimethylamino)methyl carbonyl intermediates, oxidative or Hofmann/Cope-type elimination, and access to alpha-methylenated ketones or lactones.

candidates.page_title: Eschenmoser-Claisen Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Claisen rearrangement describing Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic or benzylic alcohols heated with N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal to furnish gamma,delta-unsaturated amides through ketene N,O-acetal intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: allylic alcohol exchange with DMA-DMA, ketene aminal / ketene N,O-acetal formation, chairlike or boatlike transition-state control, and stereoselective generation of gamma,delta-unsaturated amides.

candidates.page_title: Eschenmoser-Tanabe Fragmentation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Tanabe fragmentation describing conversion of tosylhydrazones of alpha,beta-epoxy ketones into cyclic alkynones, alkynals, or acyclic alkynones by base-, acid-, or heat-induced fragmentation with loss of nitrogen.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: epoxyketone hydrazone formation, Grob-type fragmentation, release of N2 and sulfinate leaving groups, and formation of medium-sized cyclic or acyclic alkynes / alkynones from epoxy ketone precursors.

candidates.page_title: Eschweiler-Clarke Methylation (Reductive Alkylation) – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Eschweiler-Clarke methylation describing one-pot reductive methylation of primary or secondary amines with formaldehyde and formic acid, together with related reductive alkylation and cyclization variants using carbonyl compounds and reducing agents.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: iminium formation from amine and formaldehyde, hydride transfer from formic acid, selective N-methylation without overalkylation to quaternary salts, and relationship to Leuckart-Wallach-type reductive alkylation.

candidates.page_title: Evans Aldol Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Evans aldol reaction describing boron-mediated, chiral auxiliary-controlled aldol addition of N-acyl oxazolidinones to aldehydes through Z-boron enolates, delivering syn aldol adducts with high diastereoselectivity via Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Bu2BOTf-mediated enolization of oxazolidinones, Z-enolate control, Zimmerman-Traxler transition-state model, and syn-selective carbon–carbon bond formation using Evans auxiliaries.

candidates.page_title: Feist-Bénary Furan Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Feist-Bénary furan synthesis describing base-mediated condensation of beta-keto esters with alpha-halogenated carbonyl compounds to form substituted furans, often through isolable dihydrofuranol or 1,4-dicarbonyl intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: beta-keto ester enolate addition, intramolecular substitution, interrupted Feist-Bénary variants, acid-promoted dehydration, and access to highly substituted furan products.

candidates.page_title: Ferrier Reaction / Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Ferrier reaction / rearrangement describing Lewis-acid-promoted conversion of unsaturated carbohydrate derivatives into 2,3-unsaturated glycosides or highly substituted cyclohexanones through allyloxycarbenium or ketoaldehyde intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Type I Ferrier reaction, Type II Ferrier rearrangement, allyloxycarbenium formation, nucleophilic addition to glycals, and aldol-like cyclization of enol ethers to cyclohexanones.

candidates.page_title: Finkelstein Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Finkelstein reaction describing halide exchange in alkyl halides driven by differential solubility of sodium halides in polar aprotic solvents, most often exchanging chlorides or bromides for iodides under SN2 conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: sodium iodide in acetone, precipitation-driven equilibrium, SN2 halogen exchange, behavior of primary versus hindered substrates, and related conversion of sulfonates or pseudohalides.

candidates.page_title: Fischer Indole Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Fischer indole synthesis describing acid- or Lewis-acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones derived from ketones or aldehydes to furnish substituted indoles through ene-hydrazine rearrangement and ring closure.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: hydrazone formation, ene-hydrazine tautomerization, [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement, proton-transfer-driven cyclization, and the role of acidity and substitution in regiocontrol.

candidates.page_title: Fleming-Tamao Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Fleming-Tamao oxidation describing conversion of carbon-silicon bonds into alcohols under oxidative conditions through Tamao-type or Fleming-type protocols, often with retention of configuration at the carbon center.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxidative cleavage of C–Si bonds, peroxide oxidation, ipso substitution at heteroaryl silanes, hypervalent-silicon intermediates, and stereospecific access to alcohols from organosilanes.

candidates.page_title: Friedel-Crafts Acylation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Friedel-Crafts acylation describing Lewis-acid-promoted installation of acyl groups onto aromatic or heteroaromatic substrates using acyl halides, anhydrides, or related acyl donors through acylium-ion-like electrophiles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: acylium ion generation, Lewis acid activation, aromatic acylation versus aliphatic acylation, deactivation after first acylation, and utility of intramolecular variants for ring closure.

candidates.page_title: Friedel-Crafts Alkylation – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Friedel-Crafts alkylation describing Lewis- or Brønsted-acid-promoted installation of alkyl groups onto aromatic or heteroaromatic substrates from alkyl halides, alkenes, alcohols, or related electrophiles through carbocationic activation.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: catalyst-dependent carbocation formation, rearrangements of primary and secondary alkyl groups, polyalkylation, hydroarylation variants, and sensitivity to electron demand and substituent effects on the aromatic ring.

candidates.page_title: Fries, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Fries-type rearrangements describing migration of acyl groups within phenolic esters or O-aryl carbamates under Lewis-acid, photochemical, or anionic conditions to furnish ortho- or para-acylated phenols and salicylamide derivatives.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Lewis-acid-promoted acyl migration, photochemical photo-Fries rearrangement, anionic ortho-Fries via ortho-lithiated carbamates, and regioselectivity between ortho and para acylation products.
